mn1:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Ukkatthā, in the Subhaga Grove, at the root of a great sal tree.
mn1:10.2: having perceived Brahmā as Brahmā, he conceives Brahmā, he conceives in Brahmā, he conceives from Brahmā, he conceives ‘Brahmā is mine,’ he delights in Brahmā.
mn1:10.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:11.1: He perceives the Ābhassaras as the Ābhassaras;
mn1:11.2: having perceived the Ābhassaras as the Ābhassaras, he conceives the Ābhassaras, he conceives among the Ābhassaras, he conceives from the Ābhassaras, he conceives ‘the Ābhassaras are mine,’ he delights in the Ābhassaras.
mn1:11.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:114.1: The dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*] …
mn1:12.1: He perceives the Subhakiṇhas as the Subhakiṇhas;
mn1:12.2: having perceived the Subhakiṇhas as the Subhakiṇhas, he conceives the Subhakiṇhas, he conceives among the Subhakiṇhas, he conceives from the Subhakiṇhas, he conceives ‘the Subhakiṇhas are mine,’ he delights in the Subhakiṇhas.
mn1:12.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:122.2: having directly known *nibbāna* as *nibbāna*, he does not conceive *nibbāna*, he does not conceive in *nibbāna*, he does not conceive from *nibbāna*, he does not conceive ‘*nibbāna* is mine,’ he does not delight in *nibbāna*.
mn1:122.4: Because of the destruction of aversion, because he is free of aversion.
mn1:122.5: The delineation of the fifth mode-section, by way of the one with destroyed *āsavas* [*khīṇāsavavasena*], is concluded.
mn1:123.1: And that *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who is an *arahant*, with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], who has lived the holy life, done what had to be done, laid down the burden, reached his own goal, with the fetters of *bhava* entirely destroyed [*parikkhīṇabhavasaṁyojano*], completely liberated through final knowledge—he too directly knows earth as earth;
mn1:123.2: having directly known earth as earth, he does not conceive earth, he does not conceive in earth, he does not conceive from earth, he does not conceive ‘earth is mine,’ he does not delight in earth.
mn1:123.4: Because of the destruction of delusion, because he is free of delusion.
mn1:13.1: He perceives the Vehapphalas as the Vehapphalas;
mn1:13.2: having perceived the Vehapphalas as the Vehapphalas, he conceives the Vehapphalas, he conceives among the Vehapphalas, he conceives from the Vehapphalas, he conceives ‘the Vehapphalas are mine,’ he delights in the Vehapphalas.
mn1:13.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:138.1: The dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*] …
mn1:14.2: having perceived Abhibhu as Abhibhu, he conceives Abhibhu, he conceives in Abhibhu, he conceives from Abhibhu, he conceives ‘Abhibhu is mine,’ he delights in Abhibhu.
mn1:14.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:146.2: having directly known *nibbāna* as *nibbāna*, he does not conceive *nibbāna*, he does not conceive in *nibbāna*, he does not conceive from *nibbāna*, he does not conceive ‘*nibbāna* is mine,’ he does not delight in *nibbāna*.
mn1:146.4: Because of the destruction of delusion, because he is free of delusion.
mn1:146.5: The delineation of the sixth mode-section, by way of the one with destroyed *āsavas* [*khīṇāsavavasena*], is concluded.
mn1:147.1: The Tathāgata too, *bhikkhus*, an *arahant*, perfectly awakened, directly knows earth as earth;
mn1:147.2: having directly known earth as earth, he does not conceive earth, he does not conceive in earth, he does not conceive from earth, he does not conceive ‘earth is mine,’ he does not delight in earth.
mn1:147.4: I say that it has been fully understood by the Tathāgata.
mn1:15.1: He perceives the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*] as the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanato*];
mn1:15.2: having perceived the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*] as the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanato*], he conceives the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*], he conceives in the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanasmiṁ*], he conceives from the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanato*], he conceives ‘the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*] is mine,’ he delights in the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*].
mn1:15.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:16.1: He perceives the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*] as the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanato*];
mn1:16.2: having perceived the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*] as the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanato*], he conceives the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*], he conceives in the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanasmiṁ*], he conceives from the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanato*], he conceives ‘the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*] is mine,’ he delights in the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*].
mn1:16.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:162.1: The dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*] …
mn1:17.1: He perceives the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*] as the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanato*];
mn1:17.2: having perceived the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*] as the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanato*], he conceives the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*], he conceives in the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanasmiṁ*], he conceives from the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanato*], he conceives ‘the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*] is mine,’ he delights in the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*].
mn1:17.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:170.2: having directly known *nibbāna* as *nibbāna*, he does not conceive *nibbāna*, he does not conceive in *nibbāna*, he does not conceive from *nibbāna*, he does not conceive ‘*nibbāna* is mine,’ he does not delight in *nibbāna*.
mn1:170.4: I say that it has been fully understood by the Tathāgata.
mn1:170.5: The delineation of the seventh mode-section, by way of the Tathāgata, is concluded.
mn1:171.1: The Tathāgata too, *bhikkhus*, an *arahant*, perfectly awakened, directly knows earth as earth;
mn1:171.2: having directly known earth as earth, he does not conceive earth, he does not conceive in earth, he does not conceive from earth, he does not conceive ‘earth is mine,’ he does not delight in earth.
mn1:171.5: and knowing, ‘From *bhava* comes birth, and for one who has come to be, aging and death.’
mn1:171.6: Therefore, *bhikkhus*, I say that the Tathāgata has awakened to unsurpassed perfect awakening through the complete destruction, fading away, cessation, giving up, and letting go of all *taṇhās*.”
mn1:18.1: He perceives the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*] as the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanato*];
mn1:18.2: having perceived the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*] as the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanato*], he conceives the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*], he conceives in the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanasmiṁ*], he conceives from the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanato*], he conceives ‘the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*] is mine,’ he delights in the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*].
mn1:18.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:186.1: The dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*] …
mn1:19.1: He perceives what is seen as what is seen;
mn1:19.2: having perceived what is seen as what is seen, he conceives what is seen, he conceives in what is seen, he conceives from what is seen, he conceives ‘what is seen is mine,’ he delights in what is seen.
mn1:19.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:194.10: The first sutta, The Root of All Things, is concluded.
mn1:194.2: having directly known *nibbāna* as *nibbāna*, he does not conceive *nibbāna*, he does not conceive in *nibbāna*, he does not conceive from *nibbāna*, he does not conceive ‘*nibbāna* is mine,’ he does not delight in *nibbāna*.
mn1:194.5: and knowing, ‘From *bhava* comes birth, and for one who has come to be, aging and death.’
mn1:194.6: Therefore, *bhikkhus*, I say that the Tathāgata has awakened to unsurpassed perfect awakening through the complete destruction, fading away, cessation, giving up, and letting go of all *taṇhās*.”
mn1:194.7: The delineation of the eighth mode-section, by way of the Tathāgata, is concluded.
mn1:194.9: Those *bhikkhus* did not delight in what the *Bhagavā* said.
mn1:2.1: “*Bhikkhus*, I will teach you the root exposition of all *dhammas* [*sabbadhammamūlapariyāyaṁ*].
mn1:2.2: Listen to that, attend to it carefully, I will speak.”
mn1:20.1: He perceives what is heard as what is heard;
mn1:20.2: having perceived what is heard as what is heard, he conceives what is heard, he conceives in what is heard, he conceives from what is heard, he conceives ‘what is heard is mine,’ he delights in what is heard.
mn1:20.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:21.1: He perceives what is sensed as what is sensed;
mn1:21.2: having perceived what is sensed as what is sensed, he conceives what is sensed, he conceives in what is sensed, he conceives from what is sensed, he conceives ‘what is sensed is mine,’ he delights in what is sensed.
mn1:21.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:22.1: He perceives what is cognized as what is cognized;
mn1:22.2: having perceived what is cognized as what is cognized, he conceives what is cognized, he conceives in what is cognized, he conceives from what is cognized, he conceives ‘what is cognized is mine,’ he delights in what is cognized.
mn1:22.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:23.2: having perceived unity as unity, he conceives unity, he conceives in unity, he conceives from unity, he conceives ‘unity is mine,’ he delights in unity.
mn1:23.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:24.2: having perceived diversity as diversity, he conceives diversity, he conceives in diversity, he conceives from diversity, he conceives ‘diversity is mine,’ he delights in diversity.
mn1:24.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:25.2: having perceived all as all, he conceives all, he conceives in all, he conceives from all, he conceives ‘all is mine,’ he delights in all.
mn1:25.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:26.2: having perceived *nibbāna* as *nibbāna*, he conceives *nibbāna*, he conceives in *nibbāna*, he conceives from *nibbāna*, he conceives ‘*nibbāna* is mine,’ he delights in *nibbāna*.
mn1:26.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:26.5: The delineation of the first mode-section, by way of the ordinary person, is concluded.
mn1:27.1: And that *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who is a trainee, who has not yet attained his heart’s desire, dwelling aspiring to the unsurpassed security from bondage, he too directly knows earth as earth;
mn1:27.2: having directly known earth as earth, he should not conceive earth, he should not conceive in earth, he should not conceive from earth, he should not conceive ‘earth is mine,’ he should not delight in earth.
mn1:27.4: I say that it is to be fully understood by him.
mn1:3.1: “Here, *bhikkhus*, an uninstructed ordinary person, who has not seen the noble ones, who is unskilled in the noble *dhamma* [*ariyadhammassa*], untrained in the noble *dhamma* [*ariyadhamme*], who has not seen good persons, who is unskilled in the *dhamma* of good persons [*sappurisadhammassa*], untrained in the *dhamma* of good persons [*sappurisadhamme*]—
mn1:3.3: having perceived earth as earth, he conceives earth, he conceives in earth, he conceives from earth, he conceives ‘earth is mine,’ he delights in earth.
mn1:3.5: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:4.2: having perceived water as water, he conceives water, he conceives in water, he conceives from water, he conceives ‘water is mine,’ he delights in water.
mn1:4.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:42.1: The dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*] …
mn1:5.2: having perceived fire as fire, he conceives fire, he conceives in fire, he conceives from fire, he conceives ‘fire is mine,’ he delights in fire.
mn1:5.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:50.2: having directly known *nibbāna* as *nibbāna*, he should not conceive *nibbāna*, he should not conceive in *nibbāna*, he should not conceive from *nibbāna*, he should not conceive ‘*nibbāna* is mine,’ he should not delight in *nibbāna*.
mn1:50.4: I say that it is to be fully understood by him.
mn1:50.5: The delineation of the second mode-section, by way of the trainee, is concluded.
mn1:51.1: And that *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who is an *arahant*, with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], who has lived the holy life, done what had to be done, laid down the burden, reached his own goal, with the fetters of *bhava* entirely destroyed [*parikkhīṇabhavasaṁyojano*], completely liberated through final knowledge—he too directly knows earth as earth;
mn1:51.2: having directly known earth as earth, he does not conceive earth, he does not conceive in earth, he does not conceive from earth, he does not conceive ‘earth is mine,’ he does not delight in earth.
mn1:51.4: I say that it has been fully understood by him.
mn1:6.2: having perceived air as air, he conceives air, he conceives in air, he conceives from air, he conceives ‘air is mine,’ he delights in air.
mn1:6.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:66.1: The dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ*] …
mn1:7.2: having perceived beings as beings, he conceives beings, he conceives among beings, he conceives from beings, he conceives ‘beings are mine,’ he delights in beings.
mn1:7.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:74.2: having directly known *nibbāna* as *nibbāna*, he does not conceive *nibbāna*, he does not conceive in *nibbāna*, he does not conceive from *nibbāna*, he does not conceive ‘*nibbāna* is mine,’ he does not delight in *nibbāna*.
mn1:74.4: I say that it has been fully understood by him.
mn1:74.5: The delineation of the third mode-section, by way of the one with destroyed *āsavas* [*khīṇāsavavasena*], is concluded.
mn1:75.1: And that *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who is an *arahant*, with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], who has lived the holy life, done what had to be done, laid down the burden, reached his own goal, with the fetters of *bhava* entirely destroyed [*parikkhīṇabhavasaṁyojano*], completely liberated through final knowledge—he too directly knows earth as earth;
mn1:75.2: having directly known earth as earth, he does not conceive earth, he does not conceive in earth, he does not conceive from earth, he does not conceive ‘earth is mine,’ he does not delight in earth.
mn1:75.4: Because of the destruction of passion, because he is free of passion.
mn1:8.2: having perceived gods as gods, he conceives gods, he conceives among gods, he conceives from gods, he conceives ‘gods are mine,’ he delights in gods.
mn1:8.4: I say that it has not been fully understood by him.
mn1:9.2: having perceived Prajāpati as Prajāpati, he conceives Prajāpati, he conceives in Prajāpati, he conceives from Prajāpati, he conceives ‘Prajāpati is mine,’ he delights in Prajāpati.

mn1:98.4: Because of the destruction of passion, because he is free of passion.
mn1:98.5: The delineation of the fourth mode-section, by way of the one with destroyed *āsavas* [*khīṇāsavavasena*], is concluded.
mn1:99.1: And that *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who is an *arahant*, with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavo*], who has lived the holy life, done what had to be done, laid down the burden, reached his own goal, with the fetters of *bhava* entirely destroyed [*parikkhīṇabhavasaṁyojano*], completely liberated through final knowledge—he too directly knows earth as earth;
mn1:99.2: having directly known earth as earth, he does not conceive earth, he does not conceive in earth, he does not conceive from earth, he does not conceive ‘earth is mine,’ he does not delight in earth.
mn1:99.4: Because of the destruction of aversion, because he is free of aversion.
